overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active and focused presence across three social media platforms, engaging a combined follower base of 3,580. His content primarily centers around computational social science research, misinformation studies, and academic opportunities. The profile exhibits a healthy average engagement rate of approximately 5.32% across all recent posts, indicating a receptive audience for his specialized academic and research-related content.

contentThemes.description: Covers topics like new algorithms (CooRnet), methodological approaches, publication announcements on coordinated inauthentic behavior, and conference participation related to disinformation.
Includes announcements regarding new research grants, hiring postdoctoral researchers, and PhD student research opportunities.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's High Engagement: Given Bluesky's highest average engagement rate (6.14%) and the strong performance of posts announcing new publications and research opportunities, prioritize this platform for key academic announcements and direct interaction.

Strategic Cross-Posting & Platform-Specific Content: Adapt high-performing content for relevant platforms. While research updates resonate broadly, LinkedIn is ideal for formal announcements (grants, hiring) that drive professional comments, whereas Bluesky is effective for quick dissemination of new papers and conference news. Consider repurposing top-performing posts across platforms with tailored messaging.

Encourage Discussion on LinkedIn: With comments being a direct engagement metric on LinkedIn, continue to post content that invites professional discussion and networking, as demonstrated by the success of grant and hiring announcements. Actively respond to comments to foster community.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky is the most engaging platform with an average engagement rate of 6.14%. Posts announcing new publications and research opportunities perform exceptionally well, indicating it's an excellent channel for academic outreach and direct engagement within the computational social science community.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon shows consistent, moderate engagement with an average rate of 4.55%. It serves as a valuable platform for sharing research updates and fostering discussions around methodological approaches, despite having a smaller follower count compared to LinkedIn.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn boasts the largest follower base and is a strong platform for formal professional announcements, such as grant awards and hiring calls, achieving an average engagement rate of 4.87%. Content that invites comments (like hiring posts) performs well, highlighting its utility for professional networking and talent acquisition.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
